title: Silky British Eton Mess
description: A classic British dessert featuring meringue, cream, and fresh berries, given a tangy twist with Dijon mustard. This dish is perfect for a weeknight treat or special occasion.
instructions: Preheat oven to 150°C (300°F) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, whip the heavy whipping cream until soft peaks form, then set aside.
In a separate bowl, whip the meringue until stiff peaks form, set aside.

In a large serving bowl or individual glasses, create a layer of meringue, followed by a layer of whipped cream, and finally a layer of fresh berries. Repeat this process one more time, finishing with a layer of meringue on top.
Dollop the Dijon mustard on top of the meringue, followed by a sprinkle of granulated sugar and a drizzle of vanilla extract, if using.
Garnish with fresh berries and a sprinkle of granulated sugar, if desired.
Cover the dish and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Just before serving, whip the whipped cream to stiff peaks and use to top the dish, if desired.
Serve chilled, garnished with fresh berries and a sprinkle of granulated sugar, if desired.
